title: Everett moves to rein in data centers as pushback grows across Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Everett seeks to restrict data center development in the Docklands Innovation District with a new zoning proposal.
The proposal requires data centers to be part of larger developments and limits size to 20,000 square feet or five megawatts.
Debate centers on Everett's industrial history, with emphasis on environmental justice and proactive regulation against data centers.

title: South Shore med spa owner pleads guilty to administering thousands of fake Botox and other treatments
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli, a Stoughton spa owner, pleaded guilty to charges for using counterfeit Botox and fillers.
Fadanelli collected over $1 million for procedures, claiming to be a nurse, despite lacking proper licensing.
Authorities found Fadanelli imported counterfeit products from China and Brazil, conducting over 2,700 fake treatments.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about praise from Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Joe Mazzulla for the Celtics' resilience amid a slow start and roster changes this season.
Joe Mazzulla highlighted the importance of having competitive, high-character players to the Celtics' success this season.
Celtics president Brad Stevens commended the coaching staff's adaptability and strong chemistry amid roster turnover.

title: ‘That tells me a lot’: Alex Cora impressed by Trevor Story’s response to early-season adversity
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Trevor Story struggled early in the season, hitting .111 with a .333 OPS in his first nine games.
Story's recent performance included a key two-run double, helping the Red Sox win 3-2 over the Brewers.
Manager Alex Cora expressed confidence in Story's resilience after a conversation about playing time.

title: Swansea police recover 5 guns from home after report that second grader brought weapon to school
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Swansea police investigated reports of a second grader bringing a gun to Mark G. Hoyle Elementary.
Officers seized five unregistered guns from a residence, leading to pending charges against two individuals.
No guns were found at the school, but police presence will increase as a precautionary measure.

title: He grew up in R.I., named himself after Ted Williams, and is now drawing comparisons to Elvis and Roy Orbison. Meet Tyler Ballgame.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Tyler Ballgame, a Rhode Island native, is gaining attention for his debut album 'For the First Time, Again.'
Ballgame, originally Tyler Perry, attended Berklee and moved to L.A. for a fresh start in his music career.
Known for his unique sound, he draws comparisons to Elvis and Roy Orbison, showcasing dramatic indie folk-pop influences.

title: For $225k, you could live in America’s oldest shopping mall
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Arcade in Providence, America's oldest enclosed shopping mall, features 48 residential units, including a microloft for $225,000.
Unit 314 is a 265-square-foot microloft with a kitchenette, bathroom, and bedroom; HOA fee is $198.29 per month.
Listed by Lisa Jones and David Hasslinger, the unit suits students or short-term residents; it allows Airbnb usage.

title: Jayson Tatum is ‘not thrilled’ about first trip to Madison Square Garden since injury
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is set to return to Madison Square Garden for the first time after his Achilles injury 11 months ago.
Tatum, despite not being thrilled, aims to face the Knicks in New York, marking his recovery journey.
Tatum's post-injury performance includes averaging 10 rebounds per game, indicating significant improvements.

title: 5 Bruins thoughts on a winless road trip, James Hagens, and playoff matchups
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins ended their road trip 0-2-2, losing to the Hurricanes 6-5 in overtime.
Bruins' playoff odds stand at 99% with three games left against Tampa, Columbus, and New Jersey.
James Hagens may be called up to boost the Bruins' roster as they eye the playoffs.

title: When she was 11, Vanna Howard was in a refugee camp. Now she’s the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Vanna Howard is the first Cambodian American elected to the Massachusetts State Senate, representing the 1st Middlesex District.
Howard is focusing on housing, health care, and immigrant community support, driven by her refugee experience and public service career.
Howard's journey from a Thai refugee camp to U.S. public office underscores her commitment to community and overcoming adversity.
